items.notes: Registers from the login page and lands on the workspace empty state.
Clears cookies, logs in again, and returns to the workspace entry.
No direct UI logout or refresh/session persistence flow is asserted yet.

items.scenario: First workspace creation from empty state
items.notes: Creates the first workspace after user registration.
Updates the workspace name, reloads, and verifies the saved value.

items.scenario: Workspace deletion returns to empty state
items.notes: Deletes the only workspace and verifies the empty workspace page.

items.scenario: Multiple workspace creation and switcher navigation
items.notes: The sidebar workspace switcher and create-from-switcher path are not exercised.
Creates an invite from the members page and verifies the generated link.

items.scenario: Unauthenticated invitee registers and accepts invite
items.notes: Follows the invite URL, registers, returns to the invite page, and accepts.
The already-registered invitee path is not covered.

items.scenario: Invalid or expired invite token handling
items.notes: The invalid-token and expired-token UI states are not covered.

items.scenario: Accepted member appears as active in the member list
items.notes: The owner sees the accepted invitee and active status after acceptance.

items.scenario: Member list search, filters, pagination, and status actions
items.notes: The list is opened and checked, but search/filter/pagination/status actions are not asserted.

items.scenario: Member detail role, permission, resend-link, and removal flows
items.notes: The member detail route and permission editing controls are not covered.

items.scenario: Create, edit, and delete member group
items.notes: Creates a group, edits its name, then deletes it.
Adds an existing workspace member to the group and removes that member.

items.scenario: Group list search, filters, and pagination
items.notes: The group list is visited through the create flow, but list filtering and pagination are not asserted.

items.scenario: Member-role UI restrictions and denial redirects
items.notes: Owner/admin/member UI differences and guarded-route redirects need explicit e2e coverage.

items.scenario: API key create, reveal once, update, revoke, and authenticated use
items.notes: Covers the client management flow; authenticated API use is still covered by server integration tests.
